items.title: New Zealand Dollar Faces Tough Third Quarter on Growth Headwinds
items.summary: New Zealand’s dollar is facing a subdued third quarter as a resurgent greenback and the lingering effects of the Iran war’s energy shock drag on the domestic recovery.

items.title: RT by @WhiteHouse: FOR THIRTEEN MONTHS. ZERO ILLEGAL ALIENS HAVE BEEN ADMITTED TO THE UNITED STATES. All we needed was a new President—and a Secretary of War who put AMERICA FIRST.
items.summary:

items.title: trump issues a direct warning to iran stating that any escalation of conflict will lead to war
Gold Declines as Fresh US-Iran Tension Fans Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Gold declined to near $4,000 an ounce after the US and Iran traded attacks in the Persian Gulf, straining a ceasefire that had last week seen energy prices fall to pre-war levels and help temper expectations for an interest-rate hike.

items.title: No choice but to build atomic bomb : IRGC - linked media urges Iran to pursue nuclear weapon despite US deal
The World Is Racing to Develop New Nuclear Fuels
Wall Street Is Abandoning Bets on a Stronger Euro
items.summary: Wall Street banks are capitulating on bets for a stronger euro, as markets see the US outpacing Europe on interest-rate hikes for the rest of this year.

items.title: यूक्रेनी हमलों से दो और रूसी रिफायनरियों में आग लगी , पहली बार पुतिन ने माना - मुश्किल दौर में मॉस्को - putin admits russia faces difficult period amid ukraine war
Bond Heavyweights Target a Market Sweet Spot for New Warsh Era
items.summary: Some of the biggest bond managers are zeroing in on one specific area of the market as the best place to ride out the early days of the Kevin Warsh era.

items.title: Iran attacks Bahrain and Kuwait following US strikes and threatens to halt talks to end the war
US Futures Climb on Reports Peace Talks to Resume: Markets Wrap
items.summary: US equity futures climbed after reports that the US and Iran stepped back from an escalation in hostilities that had threatened the fragile ceasefire underpinning peace talks.

items.title: We Are Entering The Most Dangerous Chapter Of World War III That We Have Experienced So Far As The Agreement With Iran Collapses
The West lacks the will to fight terror while Britain faces civil war
Mission unaccomplished – Part V : US war goal of Iran zero enrichment produces zero results
With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: Iran warns ships not to bypass its chosen Hormuz route
items.summary:
Iran’s top diplomat warned on Sunday that any attempt by ships to bypass its preferred route through the Strait of Hormuz would “increase tensions” in the Middle East, as US and Iranian forces again traded attacks.
The exchanges underscored the fragility of a Pakistan-brokered agreement aimed at ending the war launched by the United States and Israel in February that disrupted shipping through the

items.title: Aramco helicopter crashes at Saudi port
items.summary: At least 14 killed in Ras Tanura incident just hours after US-Iran strikes threaten fragile deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Oil Jumps After Tanker Hit in Middle East Flare-Up
items.summary: Oil advanced after a tanker carrying Qatari crude was hit during a flare-up in attacks between the US and Iran, straining a ceasefire and hampering shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: The Path To A September Rate Cut ( Despite AI Inflation )
US and Iran Agree to Halt Attacks Ahead of Talks
items.summary: The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues, paving the way to end days of tit-for-tat attacks that tested a fragile truce. Bloomberg's Wendy Benjaminson breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)
